page.content: The data presented in Table 1 offers a comprehensive overview of the STAI-Trait measurements taken at two critical points: admission and discharge for both control subjects and individuals diagnosed with multiple sclerosis.

page.content: The table highlights the differences in anxiety levels as quantified by the State-Trait Anxiety Inventory (STAI), specifically focusing on the intervals of measurement and the corresponding results for the control group and the music intervention group. Notably, the p-value column provides insight into the statistical significance of the findings, with a particular emphasis on the comparison between the two groups across the defined intervals. With a sample size of 157 control subjects and 118 individuals with multiple sclerosis, the analysis explores the relationship between music therapy and changes in anxiety levels, as indicated by the t-Value and the Standard Error (SE) metrics. This evaluation aims to shed light on the efficacy of music as a therapeutic intervention in mitigating anxiety symptoms, thereby contributing to the broader understanding of psychological support strategies in clinical settings.
